codeReview.summary: Implementation complete. Created tracing_json.rs with JsonEvent struct containing all required fields (trace_id, span_id, timestamp, level, message, target). Added init_json_tracing() function configuring JSON-formatted output via tracing-subscriber. 3 unit tests verify serialization and log levels.

issues.description: init_json_tracing() exists but is not called from lib.rs init_tracing(). Users must explicitly call init_json_tracing() to get JSON output. This is a design choice but could use documentation in AGENTS.md to clarify when to use which initialization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md mentions 'Use tracing for structured logging' but doesn't specify JSON format requirements. The observability-structured-json feature added JSON logging, but this pattern/convention isn't documented in AGENTS.md logging section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line ~75: 'Use tracing for structured logging' - no mention of JSON format or init_json_tracing()

sharedStateObservations.observation: Rust skill (SKILL.md) covers ownership, borrowing, lifetimes but doesn't mention logging conventions or tracing-subscriber patterns. Worker correctly used tracing-subscriber fmt layer with json(), but the skill doesn't document this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Rust skill has no logging/tracing content. Worker followed standard tracing-subscriber patterns without explicit skill guidance.

summary: PASS - Implementation fully covers feature requirements. JsonEvent struct has all required fields (trace_id, span_id, timestamp, level, message). init_json_tracing() configures JSON output via tracing-subscriber. All tests pass. Build, clippy, and format checks pass. Minor non-blocking observation: init_json_tracing() not integrated with lib.rs init_tracing(), and logging conventions not documented in AGENTS.md or Rust skill.
